name: American First
description_short: American First Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving the community since 1952, offering checking, savings, loans, and business services.
description_long:
American First Credit Union was founded in 1952 with roots serving grocery store workers and has evolved into a community-focused financial institution. As a credit union, it operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model regulated by the NCUA, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The organization emphasizes financial wellness and community enrichment as core components of its mission.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts, share certificates with special interest rates, vehicle loans, home loans, credit cards, and digital banking services. They provide business solutions including business checking accounts, business savings accounts, SBA 7(a) business loans, and commercial real estate financing. Members enjoy access to 30,000 fee-free CO-OP ATMs nationwide and mobile banking capabilities available 24/7.

American First distinguishes itself through its member-centric approach and community focus. The institution offers Advantage Checking accounts with digital coupon access, share certificate specials promising guaranteed returns, and financial wellness advisors to assist with planning. The credit union emphasizes convenient digital access and has established a blog featuring financial wellness articles and community updates.

As a credit union, American First operates under NCUA insurance rather than FDIC insurance, which provides equivalent protection. Members should verify specific product terms, interest rates, and lending criteria through direct contact, as detailed rate information is not published on the website. The organization's strength lies in personalized service and member benefits typical of credit unions, though product competitiveness varies by market and individual qualification.
services: Business checking accounts (free and interest-earning options)
Business savings accounts with $50 minimum opening deposit
SBA 7(a) business loans for equipment, inventory, and debt refinancing
Commercial real estate loans for property purchase and refinancing
Advantage Checking accounts with digital coupon access
Share savings accounts and share certificate specials
Mobile banking app for 24/7 account access
Digital bill payment and peer-to-peer payment services
pros: Access to 30,000 fee-free CO-OP ATMs nationwide for convenient cash withdrawals
Share certificates with guaranteed returns and attractive interest rates as stated on website
Mobile banking app available 24/7 for account access and digital services
Advantage Checking accounts include digital coupon benefits and exclusive deals
SBA 7(a) business loans available for equipment, inventory, and debt refinancing
Commercial real estate financing for business property purchases and refinancing
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members rather than shareholders
Financial wellness advisors available to help with planning and goal-setting
cons: Specific interest rates, APRs, and loan terms not disclosed on website—requires direct inquiry
NCUA insurance (not FDIC) may be less familiar to consumers accustomed to bank insurance terminology
Limited information about membership eligibility requirements or field of membership restrictions
No transparent fee schedule published online for checking accounts, loans, or other services
Blog content is sparse with only three articles listed, suggesting limited educational resources
best_for: Community members seeking personalized service and member-owned financial institution benefits
Small business owners needing SBA loans, commercial real estate financing, or business checking
Consumers who value digital banking access and nationwide ATM networks for mobility
Members interested in share certificates and savings products with guaranteed returns
diagnosis: American First Credit Union is best for community members and small business owners seeking member-owned financial services with personalized support and nationwide ATM access. The main caveat is that specific product terms, interest rates, and fees are not publicly disclosed online, requiring direct contact to compare competitiveness against other lenders and institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: American First — Credit Union in BREA, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: American First — NCUA-insured credit union in BREA, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
